author: Marcus Aurelius
content: You have power over your mind, not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength. (Meditations, Book 6)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts. (Meditations, Book 4)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Waste no more time arguing about what a good man should be. Be one. (Meditations, Book 10)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: If it is not right, do not do it; if it is not true, do not say it. (Meditations, Book 12)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Accept the things to which fate binds you, and love the people with whom fate brings you together. (Meditations, Book 6)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Never esteem anything as of advantage to you that will make you break your word or lose your self-respect. (Meditations, Book 3)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: The best revenge is to be unlike him who performed the injury. (Meditations, Book 6)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Our life is what our thoughts make it. (Meditations, Book 4)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Do not indulge in dreams of what you do not have, but reckon up the chief of your blessings. (Meditations, Book 7)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: How much more grievous are the consequences of anger than the causes of it. (Meditations, Book 11)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Loss is nothing else but change, and change is Nature's delight. (Meditations, Book 9)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Very little is needed to make a happy life; it is all within yourself, in your way of thinking. (Meditations, Book 7)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Begin the morning by saying to yourself: I shall meet meddling, ungrateful, violent, treacherous, envious, uncharitable men today. (Meditations, Book 2)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Do what nature requires. Set out immediately, if possible; do not look around you. (Meditations, Book 8)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: A man's worth is no greater than his ambitions. (Meditations, Book 7)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: The object of life is not to be on the side of the majority, but to escape finding oneself in the ranks of the insane. (Meditations, Book 6)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Receive without pride, relinquish without struggle. (Meditations, Book 8)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Think of yourself as dead. You have lived your life. Now take what's left and live it properly. (Meditations, Book 7)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: When you wake up in the morning, tell yourself: the people I deal with today will be meddling, ungrateful, arrogant, dishonest. (Meditations, Book 2)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: If someone is able to show me that what I think or do is not right, I will happily change. (Meditations, Book 6)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way. (Meditations, Book 5)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Nothing happens to any man that he is not formed by nature to bear. (Meditations, Book 5)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Execute every act of thy life as though it were thy last. (Meditations, Book 2)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: To live happily is an inward power of the soul. (Meditations, Book 6)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Look back over the past, with its changing empires that rose and fell, and you can foresee the future. (Meditations, Book 7)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Wherever a man can live, there he can also live well. (Meditations, Book 5)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Death smiles at us all; all we can do is smile back. (Meditations, Book 6)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: He who lives in harmony with himself lives in harmony with the universe. (Meditations, Book 4)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Make the best use of what is in your power, and take the rest as it happens. (Meditations, Book 4)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Do not act as if you had ten thousand years to live. (Meditations, Book 4)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Confine yourself to the present. (Meditations, Book 8)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: The soul becomes dyed with the colour of its thoughts. (Meditations, Book 5)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: He is a wise man who does not grieve for the things which he has not, but rejoices for those which he has. (Meditations, Book 7)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Because a thing seems difficult for you, do not think it impossible for anyone to accomplish. (Meditations, Book 6)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Never let the future disturb you. You will meet it, if you have to, with the same weapons of reason which today arm you against the present. (Meditations, Book 7)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: We ought to do good to others as simply as a horse runs, or a bee makes honey, or a vine bears grapes. (Meditations, Book 5)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: It is not death that a man should fear, but he should fear never beginning to live. (Meditations, Book 9)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Everything we hear is an opinion, not a fact. Everything we see is a perspective, not the truth. (Meditations, Book 4)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Dwell on the beauty of life. Watch the stars, and see yourself running with them. (Meditations, Book 6)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: The first rule is to keep an untroubled spirit. The second is to look things in the face and know them for what they are. (Meditations, Book 8)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: It is of no use to conquer others; it is glorious to conquer oneself. (Meditations, Book 9)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: When another blames you or hates you, or people voice similar criticisms, go to their souls. (Meditations, Book 9)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: To understand the true quality of people, you must look into their minds. (Meditations, Book 8)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Whenever you are about to find fault with someone, ask yourself: what fault of mine most nearly resembles what I am about to criticize? (Meditations, Book 10)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: He who follows reason in all things has both tranquility and activity. (Meditations, Book 6)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Be tolerant with others and strict with yourself. (Meditations, Book 5)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Nowhere can man find a quieter or more untroubled retreat than in his own soul. (Meditations, Book 4)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: The true worth of a man is to be measured by the objects he pursues. (Meditations, Book 7)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Do not waste the remainder of your life in thoughts about other people. (Meditations, Book 3)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Perfection of character is this: to live each day as if it were your last, without frenzy, without apathy, without pretense. (Meditations, Book 7)

author: Seneca
content: It is not that I'm so brave, but that those who are timid are so many. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 104)

author: Seneca
content: Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 16)

author: Seneca
content: We suffer more in imagination than in reality. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 13)

author: Seneca
content: Begin at once to live, and count each separate day as a separate life. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 1)

author: Seneca
content: No man was ever wise by chance. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 76)

author: Seneca
content: The greatest obstacle to living is expectancy, which hangs upon tomorrow and loses today. (On the Shortness of Life, Chapter 9)

author: Seneca
content: A sword never kills anybody; it is a tool in the killer's hand. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 87)

author: Seneca
content: Difficulties strengthen the mind, as labour does the body. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 78)

author: Seneca
content: If you wished to be loved, love. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 9)

author: Seneca
content: True happiness is to enjoy the present, without anxious dependence upon the future. (On the Happy Life, Chapter 3)

author: Seneca
content: As is a tale, so is life: not how long it is, but how good it is, is what matters. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 77)

author: Seneca
content: He who is brave is free. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 113)

author: Seneca
content: Omnia aliena sunt, tempus tantum nostrum est — All things are alien to us; time alone is ours. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 1)

author: Seneca
content: Nusquam est qui ubique est — He who is everywhere is nowhere. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 2)

author: Seneca
content: The time will come when diligent research over long periods will bring to light things which now lie hidden. (Natural Questions, Book 7)

author: Seneca
content: Retire into yourself as much as possible. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 7)

author: Seneca
content: Inimica est multorum conversatio — The company of many people is harmful. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 7)

author: Seneca
content: It is not the man who has too little, but the man who craves more, that is poor. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 2)

author: Seneca
content: The whole future lies in uncertainty: live immediately. (On the Shortness of Life, Chapter 9)

author: Seneca
content: If you want to be loved, love. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 9)

author: Seneca
content: Vindica te tibi — Claim yourself for yourself. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 1)

author: Seneca
content: There is nothing the busy man is less busied with than living. (On the Shortness of Life, Chapter 1)

author: Seneca
content: Philosophy promises above all: common sense, humanity, and fellowship. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 5)

author: Seneca
content: Nothing, Lucilius, is ours except time. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 1)

author: Seneca
content: Nemo potest personam diu gerere — No man can wear a mask for long. (On Clemency, Book 1)

author: Seneca
content: One hand washes the other. (Apocolocyntosis, Chapter 9)

author: Seneca
content: If you are wise, mingle these two elements: do not hope without despair, or despair without hope. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 104)

author: Seneca
content: Treat your inferiors as you would be treated by your betters. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 47)

author: Seneca
content: The man who has anticipated the coming of troubles takes away their power when they arrive. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 98)

author: Seneca
content: We are more often frightened than hurt, and we suffer more in imagination than in reality. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 13)

author: Seneca
content: While we wait for life, life passes. (On the Shortness of Life, Chapter 1)

author: Seneca
content: Per aspera ad astra — Through hardships to the stars. (Hercules Furens, Act 2)

author: Seneca
content: Do not ask for what you will wish you had not got. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 95)

author: Seneca
content: He who fears death will never do anything worthy of a living man. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 82)

author: Seneca
content: A great mind becomes a great fortune. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 5)

author: Seneca
content: Non ad hoc amicos quaerit ut habeat qui sibi aegro assideat — We do not seek friends so they will help us when sick, but so we may help them. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 9)

author: Seneca
content: The good of the spirit grows with the years. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 12)

author: Seneca
content: Recede in te ipse quantum potes — Withdraw into yourself as much as you can. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 7)

author: Seneca
content: It is quality rather than quantity that matters. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 45)

author: Seneca
content: The soul that sees beauty may sometimes walk alone. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 41)

author: Seneca
content: Life is long, if you know how to use it. (On the Shortness of Life, Chapter 1)

author: Seneca
content: When I think over what I have said, I envy dumb people. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 40)

author: Seneca
content: Docendo discimus — We learn by teaching. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 7)

author: Seneca
content: Otium sine literis mors est — Leisure without literature is death. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 82)

author: Seneca
content: Associate with those who are likely to make you better. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 7)

author: Seneca
content: What need is there to weep over parts of life? The whole of it calls for tears. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 99)

author: Seneca
content: I shall never be ashamed of citing a bad author if the line is good. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 33)

author: Seneca
content: To be everywhere is to be nowhere. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 2)

author: Seneca
content: Impedit ira animum — Anger impedes the mind. (On Anger, Book 3)

author: Seneca
content: That you may not suffer fear in adversity, think before it comes of everything. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 107)

author: Seneca
content: Sero in antiquum limen — It is too late to take up old habits. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 1)

author: Aristotle
content: Happiness depends upon ourselves. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 1, Chapter 9)

author: Aristotle
content: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 2, Chapter 1)

author: Aristotle
content: The whole is more than the sum of its parts. (Metaphysics, Book 8, Chapter 6)

author: Aristotle
content: Knowing yourself is the beginning of all wisdom. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 9, Chapter 8)

author: Aristotle
content: Man is by nature a political animal. (Politics, Book 1, Chapter 2)

author: Aristotle
content: The educated differ from the uneducated as much as the living from the dead. (Lives of the Eminent Philosophers, via Diogenes Laertius)

author: Aristotle
content: The more you know, the more you know you don't know. (Metaphysics, Book 2, Chapter 1)

author: Aristotle
content: Those who educate children well are more to be honored than they who produce them. (Rhetoric, Book 1, Chapter 5)

author: Aristotle
content: The antidote for fifty enemies is one friend. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 9, Chapter 4)

author: Aristotle
content: Good habits formed at youth make all the difference. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 2, Chapter 1)

author: Aristotle
content: Courage is the first of human qualities because it is the quality which guarantees the others. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 3, Chapter 6)

author: Aristotle
content: The worst form of inequality is to try to make unequal things equal. (Politics, Book 3, Chapter 9)

author: Aristotle
content: To perceive is to suffer. (De Anima, Book 2, Chapter 5)

author: Aristotle
content: Education is the best provision for old age. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 9)

author: Aristotle
content: At his best, man is the noblest of all animals; separated from law and justice he is the worst. (Politics, Book 1, Chapter 2)

author: Aristotle
content: The aim of the wise is not to secure pleasure, but to avoid pain. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 7, Chapter 11)

author: Aristotle
content: Pleasure in the job puts perfection in the work. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 10, Chapter 5)

author: Aristotle
content: A friend is a second self. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 9, Chapter 4)

author: Aristotle
content: No great mind has ever existed without a touch of madness. (Problem XXX, Chapter 1)

author: Aristotle
content: Hope is a waking dream. (Diogenes Laertius, Lives, Book 5)

author: Aristotle
content: Fear is pain arising from the anticipation of evil. (Rhetoric, Book 2, Chapter 5)

author: Aristotle
content: Quality is not an act; it is a habit. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 2, Chapter 4)

author: Aristotle
content: We cannot learn without pain. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 10, Chapter 1)

author: Aristotle
content: The secret to humor is surprise. (Rhetoric, Book 3, Chapter 11)

author: Aristotle
content: The high-minded man must care more for truth than for what people think. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 4, Chapter 3)

author: Aristotle
content: It is the mark of an educated mind to be able to entertain a thought without accepting it. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 1, Chapter 3)

author: Aristotle
content: Without friends, no one would want to live, even if he had all other goods. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 8, Chapter 1)

author: Aristotle
content: Excellence is never an accident; it is the result of high intention and skillful execution. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 2, Chapter 6)

author: Aristotle
content: The law is reason free from passion. (Politics, Book 3, Chapter 16)

author: Aristotle
content: Nature does nothing in vain. (Politics, Book 1, Chapter 2)

author: Aristotle
content: All virtue is summed up in dealing justly. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 5, Chapter 1)

author: Aristotle
content: Even when laws have been written down, they ought not always to remain unaltered. (Politics, Book 2, Chapter 8)

author: Aristotle
content: The basis of a democratic state is liberty. (Politics, Book 6, Chapter 2)

author: Aristotle
content: Poetry is finer and more philosophical than history. (Poetics, Chapter 9)

author: Aristotle
content: It is not enough to win a war; it is more important to organize the peace. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 10, Chapter 7)

author: Aristotle
content: The things that come to us by nature are not virtue or vice, but the material of virtue and vice. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 2, Chapter 1)

author: Aristotle
content: Emotional intelligence begins developing in the womb. (De Anima, Book 3, Chapter 9)

author: Aristotle
content: Character may almost be called the most effective means of persuasion. (Rhetoric, Book 1, Chapter 2)

author: Aristotle
content: Men acquire a particular quality by constantly acting in a particular way. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 2, Chapter 1)

author: Aristotle
content: Happiness is a state of activity. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 1, Chapter 8)

author: Aristotle
content: Comedy aims at representing men as worse, tragedy as better than in actual life. (Poetics, Chapter 2)

author: Aristotle
content: Music has the power of producing a certain effect on the moral character of the soul. (Politics, Book 8, Chapter 5)

author: Aristotle
content: The ideal man bears the accidents of life with dignity. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 1, Chapter 10)

author: Aristotle
content: A constitution is the arrangement of magistracies in a state. (Politics, Book 4, Chapter 1)

author: Aristotle
content: Men become builders by building and lyreplayers by playing the lyre. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 2, Chapter 1)

author: Aristotle
content: The wise man does not expose himself needlessly to danger. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 4, Chapter 3)

author: Aristotle
content: A body in motion is moved either by itself or by something else. (Physics, Book 7, Chapter 1)

author: Aristotle
content: Piety requires us to honor truth above our friends. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 1, Chapter 6)

author: Aristotle
content: The greatest virtues are those which are most useful to other persons. (Rhetoric, Book 1, Chapter 9)

author: Aristotle
content: Every art and every inquiry, and similarly every action and pursuit, is thought to aim at some good. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 1, Chapter 1)

author: Aristotle
content: Equity is that idea of justice which contravenes the written law. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 5, Chapter 10)

author: Plato
content: The beginning is the most important part of the work. (The Republic, Book 2)

author: Plato
content: Wise men speak because they have something to say; fools because they have to say something. (Phaedrus, 276a)

author: Plato
content: At the touch of love, everyone becomes a poet. (The Symposium, 196e)

author: Plato
content: Knowledge is the food of the soul. (Protagoras, 313c)

author: Plato
content: The measure of a man is what he does with power. (The Republic, Book 1)

author: Plato
content: We can easily forgive a child who is afraid of the dark; the real tragedy of life is when men are afraid of the light. (Phaedo, 83a)

author: Plato
content: Music is a moral law. It gives soul to the universe, wings to the mind, flight to the imagination. (The Republic, Book 3)

author: Plato
content: One of the penalties for refusing to participate in politics is that you end up being governed by your inferiors. (The Republic, Book 1)

author: Plato
content: Human behavior flows from three main sources: desire, emotion, and knowledge. (The Republic, Book 4)

author: Plato
content: The price good men pay for indifference to public affairs is to be ruled by evil men. (The Republic, Book 1)

author: Plato
content: Ignorance, the root and stem of all evil. (Timaeus, 86d)

author: Plato
content: Good people do not need laws to tell them to act responsibly, while bad people will find a way around the laws. (The Republic, Book 4)

author: Plato
content: There are three classes of men: lovers of wisdom, lovers of honor, lovers of gain. (The Republic, Book 9)

author: Plato
content: No man should bring children into the world who is unwilling to persevere to the end in their nature and education. (The Republic, Book 4)

author: Plato
content: You can discover more about a person in an hour of play than in a year of conversation. (The Republic, Book 1)

author: Plato
content: The greatest wealth is to live content with little. (The Republic, Book 4)

author: Plato
content: Every heart sings a song, incomplete, until another heart whispers back. (The Symposium, 192e)

author: Plato
content: Opinion is the medium between knowledge and ignorance. (The Republic, Book 5, 478d)

author: Plato
content: The true lover of knowledge is always striving after being. (The Republic, Book 6, 490a)

author: Plato
content: Do not train a child to learn by force or harshness; but direct them to it by what amuses their minds. (The Republic, Book 2)

author: Plato
content: Courage is knowing what not to fear. (Protagoras, 360d)

author: Plato
content: Excess generally causes reaction, and produces a change in the opposite direction, whether it be in the seasons, in individuals, or in governments. (The Republic, Book 8)

author: Plato
content: The soul takes nothing with her to the next world but her education and her culture. (The Republic, Book 10, 618b)

author: Plato
content: Books are immortal sons defying their sires. (Phaedrus, 275a)

author: Plato
content: I am the wisest man alive, for I know one thing, and that is that I know nothing. (The Apology of Socrates, 21d)

author: Plato
content: The direction in which education starts a man will determine his future life. (The Republic, Book 4, 425b)

author: Plato
content: He who commits injustice is ever made more wretched than he who suffers it. (Gorgias, 479d)

author: Plato
content: No law or ordinance is mightier than understanding. (Letters, Letter 7, 354e)

author: Plato
content: The heaviest penalty for declining to rule is to be ruled by someone inferior to yourself. (The Republic, Book 1, 347c)

author: Plato
content: Love is a serious mental disease. (Phaedrus, 265a)

author: Plato
content: Philosophy is the highest music. (Phaedo, 61a)

author: Plato
content: Excellence is not a gift, but a skill that takes practice. (Meno, 88d)

author: Plato
content: Self-conquest is the greatest of all victories. (Laws, Book 1, 626e)

author: Plato
content: The learning and knowledge that we have is at the most but little compared with that of which we are ignorant. (Theaetetus, 155d)

author: Plato
content: There is no harm in repeating a good thing. (Phaedrus, 227b)

author: Plato
content: Only the dead have seen the end of war. (The Republic, Book 5, 469a)

author: Plato
content: Justice in the life and conduct of the state is possible only as first it resides in the hearts of citizens. (The Republic, Book 4, 434c)

author: Plato
content: The function of education is to help you understand the importance of doing right things. (The Republic, Book 7, 519e)

author: Plato
content: Truth is its own reward. (Phaedo, 114d)

author: Plato
content: Necessity is the mother of invention. (The Republic, Book 2, 369c)

author: Plato
content: All learning has an emotional base. (Meno, 81a)

author: Plato
content: A good decision is based on knowledge and not on numbers. (Phaedrus, 260a)

author: Plato
content: We do not learn, and what we call learning is only a process of recollection. (Meno, 81c)

author: Plato
content: Rhetoric is the art of ruling the minds of men. (Phaedrus, 261a)

author: Plato
content: Thinking is the talking of the soul with itself. (Theaetetus, 189e)

author: Plato
content: The safest general characterization of philosophy is that it consists of a series of footnotes to Plato. (Parmenides, introduction)

author: Plato
content: Good actions give strength to ourselves and inspire good actions in others. (The Republic, Book 4, 444e)

author: Plato
content: Any man may easily do harm, but not every man can do good to another. (The Republic, Book 1, 335e)

author: Plato
content: To do injustice is the greatest of all evils. (Gorgias, 469b)

author: Plato
content: Let parents bequeath to their children not riches, but the spirit of reverence. (Laws, Book 11, 927b)

author: Plato
content: Man never legislates, but destinies and accidents, happening in all sorts of ways, legislate in all sorts of ways. (Laws, Book 4, 709a)

author: The Bible
content: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth. (Genesis 1:1)

author: The Bible
content: The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want. (Psalm 23:1)

author: The Bible
content: For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son. (John 3:16)

author: The Bible
content: I can do all this through him who gives me strength. (Philippians 4:13)

author: The Bible
content: Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding. (Proverbs 3:5)

author: The Bible
content: Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged, for the Lord your God will be with you. (Joshua 1:9)

author: The Bible
content: The Lord is my light and my salvation — whom shall I fear? (Psalm 27:1)

author: The Bible
content: Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud. (1 Corinthians 13:4)

author: The Bible
content: Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, present your requests to God. (Philippians 4:6)

author: The Bible
content: For I know the plans I have for you, plans to prosper you and not to harm you, to give you hope and a future. (Jeremiah 29:11)

author: The Bible
content: And we know that in all things God works for the good of those who love him. (Romans 8:28)

author: The Bible
content: Be still, and know that I am God. (Psalm 46:10)

author: The Bible
content: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom. (Proverbs 9:10)

author: The Bible
content: Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. (Matthew 11:28)

author: The Bible
content: Ask and it will be given to you; seek and you will find; knock and the door will be opened to you. (Matthew 7:7)

author: The Bible
content: The truth will set you free. (John 8:32)

author: The Bible
content: A gentle answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger. (Proverbs 15:1)

author: The Bible
content: Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me. (Psalm 23:4)

author: The Bible
content: Do to others as you would have them do to you. (Luke 6:31)

author: The Bible
content: For it is by grace you have been saved, through faith — not by works, so that no one can boast. (Ephesians 2:8–9)

author: The Bible
content: In all your ways submit to him, and he will make your paths straight. (Proverbs 3:6)

author: The Bible
content: This is the day the Lord has made; let us rejoice and be glad in it. (Psalm 118:24)

author: The Bible
content: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also. (Matthew 6:21)

author: The Bible
content: Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. (Joshua 1:9)

author: The Bible
content: The Lord your God is with you, the Mighty Warrior who saves. (Zephaniah 3:17)

author: The Bible
content: Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good; his love endures forever. (Psalm 107:1)

author: The Bible
content: He gives strength to the weary and increases the power of the weak. (Isaiah 40:29)

author: The Bible
content: There is no fear in love. But perfect love drives out fear. (1 John 4:18)

author: The Bible
content: Whatever you do, work at it with all your heart, as working for the Lord. (Colossians 3:23)

author: The Bible
content: The name of the Lord is a fortified tower; the righteous run to it and are safe. (Proverbs 18:10)

author: The Bible
content: Do not conform to the pattern of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind. (Romans 12:2)

author: The Bible
content: Delight yourself in the Lord, and he will give you the desires of your heart. (Psalm 37:4)

author: The Bible
content: No discipline seems pleasant at the time, but painful. Later on, however, it produces a harvest of righteousness. (Hebrews 12:11)

author: The Bible
content: Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it. (Proverbs 4:23)

author: The Bible
content: Jesus wept. (John 11:35)

author: The Bible
content: For God did not give us a spirit of timidity, but a spirit of power, of love and of self-discipline. (2 Timothy 1:7)

author: The Bible
content: Create in me a pure heart, O God, and renew a steadfast spirit within me. (Psalm 51:10)

author: The Bible
content: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God. (John 1:1)

author: The Bible
content: Blessed are the pure in heart, for they will see God. (Matthew 5:8)

author: The Bible
content: Let your light shine before others, that they may see your good deeds. (Matthew 5:16)

author: The Bible
content: Do not let the sun go down while you are still angry. (Ephesians 4:26)

author: The Bible
content: The heart is deceitful above all things. Who can understand it? (Jeremiah 17:9)

author: The Bible
content: My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness. (2 Corinthians 12:9)

author: The Bible
content: Cast all your anxiety on him because he cares for you. (1 Peter 5:7)

author: The Bible
content: I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me. (John 14:6)

author: The Bible
content: You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind. (Matthew 22:37)

author: The Bible
content: Whoever wants to be my disciple must deny themselves and take up their cross and follow me. (Mark 8:34)

author: The Bible
content: A friend loves at all times, and a brother is born for a time of adversity. (Proverbs 17:17)

author: The Bible
content: The Lord is close to the brokenhearted and saves those who are crushed in spirit. (Psalm 34:18)

author: The Bible
content: Pride goes before destruction, a haughty spirit before a fall. (Proverbs 16:18)

author: The Bible
content: Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God. (Matthew 5:9)

author: The Bible
content: Surely goodness and love will follow me all the days of my life. (Psalm 23:6)

author: The Bible
content: For the wages of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord. (Romans 6:23)

author: The Bible
content: Hear, O Israel: The Lord our God, the Lord is one. (Deuteronomy 6:4)

author: The Bible
content: Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come. (2 Corinthians 5:17)

author: The Bible
content: The Lord bless you and keep you; the Lord make his face shine on you. (Numbers 6:24–25)

author: The Bible
content: All Scripture is God-breathed and is useful for teaching, rebuking, correcting, and training in righteousness. (2 Timothy 3:16)

author: The Bible
content: Love your neighbor as yourself. (Mark 12:31)

author: The Bible
content: Now faith is confidence in what we hope for and assurance about what we do not see. (Hebrews 11:1)

author: The Bible
content: But those who hope in the Lord will renew their strength. They will soar on wings like eagles. (Isaiah 40:31)

author: The Bible
content: The Lord is my strength and my shield; my heart trusts in him, and he helps me. (Psalm 28:7)

author: The Bible
content: Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good. (Romans 12:21)

author: The Bible
content: For I am convinced that neither death nor life can separate us from the love of God. (Romans 8:38–39)

author: The Bible
content: Seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well. (Matthew 6:33)

author: The Bible
content: As iron sharpens iron, so one person sharpens another. (Proverbs 27:17)

author: The Bible
content: Rejoice always, pray continually, give thanks in all circumstances. (1 Thessalonians 5:16–18)

author: The Bible
content: And now these three remain: faith, hope and love. But the greatest of these is love. (1 Corinthians 13:13)

author: The Bible
content: Yet to all who did receive him, to those who believed in his name, he gave the right to become children of God. (John 1:12)

author: The Bible
content: How good and pleasant it is when God's people live together in unity. (Psalm 133:1)

author: The Bible
content: Commit to the Lord whatever you do, and he will establish your plans. (Proverbs 16:3)

author: The Bible
content: Your word is a lamp for my feet, a light on my path. (Psalm 119:105)

author: The Bible
content: If my people, who are called by my name, will humble themselves and pray, I will forgive their sin. (2 Chronicles 7:14)

author: The Bible
content: You were bought at a price. Therefore honor God with your bodies. (1 Corinthians 6:20)

author: The Bible
content: Blessed are those who mourn, for they will be comforted. (Matthew 5:4)

author: The Bible
content: The Lord is my rock, my fortress and my deliverer. (Psalm 18:2)

author: The Bible
content: Whatever is true, whatever is noble, whatever is right — think about such things. (Philippians 4:8)

author: The Bible
content: I lift up my eyes to the mountains — where does my help come from? My help comes from the Lord. (Psalm 121:1–2)

author: The Bible
content: Peace I leave with you; my peace I give you. I do not give to you as the world gives. (John 14:27)

author: The Bible
content: Even the sparrow has found a home, and the swallow a nest for herself. (Psalm 84:3)

author: The Bible
content: Teach us to number our days, that we may gain a heart of wisdom. (Psalm 90:12)

author: The Bible
content: He has shown you, O mortal, what is good. And what does the Lord require of you? To act justly and to love mercy. (Micah 6:8)

author: The Bible
content: Do not judge, or you too will be judged. (Matthew 7:1)

author: The Bible
content: The righteous cry out, and the Lord hears them; he delivers them from all their troubles. (Psalm 34:17)

author: The Bible
content: Weeping may stay for the night, but rejoicing comes in the morning. (Psalm 30:5)

author: The Bible
content: Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest. (Galatians 6:9)

author: The Bible
content: I have learned, in whatsoever state I am, therewith to be content. (Philippians 4:11)

author: The Bible
content: The Lord is my strength and song, and he is become my salvation. (Exodus 15:2)

author: The Bible
content: Love does not delight in evil but rejoices with the truth. (1 Corinthians 13:6)

author: The Bible
content: Blessed is the one who does not walk in step with the wicked. (Psalm 1:1)

author: The Bible
content: He makes me lie down in green pastures, he leads me beside quiet waters. (Psalm 23:2)

author: The Bible
content: Everyone who asks receives; the one who seeks finds. (Matthew 7:8)

author: The Bible
content: Whoever does not love does not know God, because God is love. (1 John 4:8)

author: The Bible
content: The earth is the Lord's, and everything in it, the world, and all who live in it. (Psalm 24:1)

author: The Bible
content: The Lord is my portion; therefore I will wait for him. (Lamentations 3:24)

author: The Bible
content: A cheerful heart is good medicine, but a crushed spirit dries up the bones. (Proverbs 17:22)

author: The Bible
content: He restores my soul. He guides me along the right paths for his name's sake. (Psalm 23:3)

author: The Bible
content: Come near to God and he will come near to you. (James 4:8)

author: The Bible
content: If any of you lacks wisdom, you should ask God, who gives generously to all. (James 1:5)

author: The Bible
content: The Lord is gracious and compassionate, slow to anger and rich in love. (Psalm 145:8)

author: The Bible
content: Do not store up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moths and vermin destroy. (Matthew 6:19)

author: The Bible
content: My strength is dried up like a potsherd; and my tongue cleaveth to my jaws. (Psalm 22:15)

author: The Bible
content: Honor your father and your mother, so that you may live long. (Exodus 20:12)

author: The Bible
content: For where two or three gather in my name, there am I with them. (Matthew 18:20)

author: The Bible
content: You shall not murder. (Exodus 20:13)

author: The Bible
content: Blessed are the meek, for they will inherit the earth. (Matthew 5:5)

author: The Bible
content: So do not fear, for I am with you; do not be dismayed, for I am your God. (Isaiah 41:10)

author: The Bible
content: I will praise you, Lord, with all my heart; I will tell of all the marvelous things you have done. (Psalm 9:1)

author: The Bible
content: All have sinned and fall short of the glory of God. (Romans 3:23)

author: The Bible
content: Therefore do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will worry about itself. (Matthew 6:34)

author: The Bible
content: I am with you always, to the very end of the age. (Matthew 28:20)

author: The Bible
content: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly. (Colossians 3:16)

author: The Bible
content: Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be filled. (Matthew 5:6)

author: The Bible
content: God is our refuge and strength, an ever-present help in trouble. (Psalm 46:1)

author: The Bible
content: You are the light of the world. A town built on a hill cannot be hidden. (Matthew 5:14)

author: The Bible
content: The Lord will fight for you; you need only to be still. (Exodus 14:14)

author: The Bible
content: Great is your faithfulness. (Lamentations 3:23)

author: The Bible
content: The Lord is not slow in keeping his promise, but is patient with you. (2 Peter 3:9)

author: The Bible
content: Nothing is impossible with God. (Luke 1:37)

author: The Bible
content: Walk by faith, not by sight. (2 Corinthians 5:7)

author: The Bible
content: The Lord your God is a consuming fire, a jealous God. (Deuteronomy 4:24)

author: The Bible
content: Taste and see that the Lord is good; blessed is the one who takes refuge in him. (Psalm 34:8)

author: The Bible
content: New mercies from the Lord I see every morning. (Lamentations 3:22–23)

author: The Bible
content: Those who hope in the Lord will renew their strength. (Isaiah 40:31)

author: The Bible
content: Let us love one another, for love comes from God. (1 John 4:7)

author: The Bible
content: From everyone who has been given much, much will be demanded. (Luke 12:48)

author: The Bible
content: The Lord watches over you; the Lord is your shade at your right hand. (Psalm 121:5)

author: The Bible
content: Blessed is the one who trusts in the Lord, whose confidence is in him. (Jeremiah 17:7)

author: The Bible
content: Forgive us our debts, as we also have forgiven our debtors. (Matthew 6:12)

author: The Bible
content: Your righteousness is like the highest mountains, your justice like the great deep. (Psalm 36:6)

author: The Bible
content: The name of the Lord is a strong tower; the righteous run to it and are safe. (Proverbs 18:10)

author: The Bible
content: Blessed are those who are persecuted because of righteousness, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven. (Matthew 5:10)

author: The Bible
content: Do not be afraid of those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul. (Matthew 10:28)

author: The Bible
content: I am the resurrection and the life. The one who believes in me will live, even though they die. (John 11:25)

author: The Bible
content: The Lord has done great things for us, and we are filled with joy. (Psalm 126:3)

author: The Bible
content: When I am weak, then I am strong. (2 Corinthians 12:10)

author: The Bible
content: I praise you because I am fearfully and wonderfully made. (Psalm 139:14)

author: The Bible
content: Plans fail for lack of counsel, but with many advisers they succeed. (Proverbs 15:22)

author: The Bible
content: The Lord detests lying lips, but he delights in people who are trustworthy. (Proverbs 12:22)

author: The Bible
content: Teach a child in the way he should go, and when he is old he will not turn from it. (Proverbs 22:6)

author: The Bible
content: Two are better than one, because they have a good return for their labor. (Ecclesiastes 4:9)

author: The Bible
content: There is a time for everything, and a season for every activity under the heavens. (Ecclesiastes 3:1)

author: The Bible
content: I am the vine; you are the branches. (John 15:5)

author: The Bible
content: God is not human, that he should lie, not a human being, that he should change his mind. (Numbers 23:19)

author: The Bible
content: The Spirit of the Sovereign Lord is on me, because the Lord has anointed me to proclaim good news to the poor. (Isaiah 61:1)

author: The Bible
content: The Lord is slow to anger, abounding in love and forgiving sin. (Numbers 14:18)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Your days are numbered. Use them to throw open the windows of your soul to the sun. (Meditations, Book 8)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Think of all the years passed by in which you said to yourself 'I'll do it tomorrow.' (Meditations, Book 8)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: How much time he gains who does not look to see what his neighbour says or does or thinks. (Meditations, Book 4)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way. (Meditations, Book 5)

author: Marcus Aurelius
content: Reject your sense of injury and the injury itself disappears. (Meditations, Book 4)

author: Seneca
content: Nusquam est qui ubique est — To be everywhere is to be nowhere. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 2)

author: Seneca
content: Errare humanum est — To err is human. (On the Shortness of Life, Chapter 11)

author: Seneca
content: Dum differtur vita transcurrit — While we are postponing, life speeds by. (Letters to Lucilius, Letter 1)

author: Aristotle
content: Happiness is the meaning and the purpose of life, the whole aim and end of human existence. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 1, Chapter 7)

author: Aristotle
content: Wicked men obey from fear; good men from love. (Nicomachean Ethics, Book 8, Chapter 4)

author: Aristotle
content: Poverty is the parent of revolution and crime. (Politics, Book 2, Chapter 6)

author: Plato
content: Wonder is the beginning of wisdom. (Theaetetus, 155d)

author: Plato
content: The unexamined life is not worth living. (The Apology of Socrates, 38a)

author: Plato
content: You should not honor men more than truth. (The Republic, Book 10, 595c)

author: The Bible
content: My God, my God, why have you forsaken me? (Psalm 22:1)

author: The Bible
content: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and today and forever. (Hebrews 13:8)

author: The Bible
content: We love because he first loved us. (1 John 4:19)

author: The Bible
content: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. (Philippians 4:23)

author: The Bible
content: Set your minds on things above, not on earthly things. (Colossians 3:2)
